# Transcrição: 02 - Referral Based Programs

- **Fonte:** 02 - Referral Based Programs
- **Duração:** 17:27
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

## [00:00 - 03:00]

Alright guys, in today's workshop I'm going to take you through referrals, nice juicy referrals, otherwise known as the pot of gold at the end of the rainbow for a lot of us. So if you're like most Facebook marketers and agency owners, a lot of your business was dependent on this type of traffic or leads, I should say referral based leads. And before you automated your lead generation system, most of your clients, your existing clients would have come through this method. Now the reason we love referrals so much, and honestly I probably don't even need to cover this with you guys, you know this already, is that they're very high quality in the sense that they have a much higher close rate. So sometimes you'll talk to people and hear about 90% close rates, well those are typically word of mouth referral based leads, so very very high close rates. Makes sense, if you think about it, your client is essentially advocating for your agency talking about how, you know, hey they're getting great results or they're really enjoying working with you and you should check out this agency if you need help. So it builds a lot of authority, it builds a lot of credibility and it builds a lot of that trust right from the get go. So the sales process is a lot easier as a consequence. In fact it's almost unusual to need or to require a two phone close with these guys, a lot of referrals just need one phone call closes. Now you still need to do your due diligence and make sure it's the right fit and setting the right expectations. However the sales process is definitely a lot higher and they typically have a higher tolerance for high prices. And a lot of that is purely because they've discussed that with the client, they referred them right, the client would have said yeah you know we spent 10k but we made that money back so they have some kind of bearing or idea on what the price is. If not that, it's because they're seeing the results your client is getting and they want a piece of that pie and their understanding of that price is worth it. Now usually when we talk about referrals we're referring, no pun intended, to word of mouth. So word of mouth leads your client actively talking about it and suggesting or advocating for you. However testimonials are also a form of referral and you can use testimonials very actively or aggressively inside of your marketing especially your automated lead gen marketing funnels.

## [03:00 - 06:06]

So those are very valuable as well and I'm going to touch on testimonials as well in this training. Now the cons of referrals on the flip side of that is that as you guys know they are very unpredictable. It is very hard to scale and build a business off purely off of referrals simply as you just never know when the next one's coming through. And something else to consider is that they also potentially could cause some friction between you and the original client. So if for whatever reason the relationship goes sour between you and the referred client that can sometimes spill over into that relationship with your original client especially if that referred client was a really close friend or a family member of your client. So it's just something to be aware of just like with any lead make sure you set the right expectations make sure you audit them properly all very important steps that you shouldn't neglect just because it's a referral. So if we think about it what are the reasons that a client is going to refer you. So what are the reasons that someone's going to go out there and actively talk about the agency and there's a couple reasons for it. The first one is obviously they're getting you're crushing their results right or they love working with you. Notice how I say or it's actually and or so even sometimes when you're not crushing results clients will refer you because they just the customer service is so great right. So communication goes a really long ways guys with retention and loyalty of clients don't take that for granted. Clients will stick with you for a long time even through really bad months of campaigns if there's great open communication. So the most obvious one is you're getting great results or they love working with you and they're just raving about you to people that might need help. The second one is they may have networks or be well connected. So sometimes you're going to hear from leads where they're saying to you hey you know I am a coach and I've got lots of students and my students all need Facebook ads agency or Facebook ads freelancer and I'm going to refer you to all of them once you get results or you know I'm on the board of directors for all these companies and I'm going to I'm going to share you out if you can get these results. So that's the second one and then the third reason is there's an incentive. This is a very common strategy. So if you look at companies like ClickFunnels and a lot of those SAS companies they pretty much built their entire businesses off referrals otherwise known as affiliates. It's very very similar. Now with number one and two results from campaigns is obviously something we focus on quite heavily

## [06:06 - 09:10]

inside of the academy but with number two there's not much that you can do about this right. It's sort of out of your control how well connected your client is where you do have control is number three. So creating a referral based incentive program inside of your agency. So let's have a look at how we do it at eight loop. Now before I do I just want to point out that referral incentives do not have to mean you're giving them a Ferrari or some I don't even know what kind of car that is but some flash looking car. Yes if a client sending you hundreds of thousands of dollars every single month absolutely feel free to do that. But the way that we do it is quite simple. So we have either we use this interchangeably and we make the call based on the value of the client. So the monthly invoicing value of that client. So we will either do twelve hundred dollars off the monthly invoice for every referred client or we'll do 20 percent off the monthly invoice for every referred client. Now if you think about it a five K client is going to be one thousand dollars at the twenty percent mark. So that's a great option for that. However if your client is a 10 K a month client or 20 K a month client you probably want to opt for the twelve hundred dollar option. And this is important. This is just suggestion. So this is what we do. This is the number that works with us. This is the number that works with our clients and sometimes we will even change those numbers depending on the client and how valuable their leads and referrals have been or how aggressive they are with it. So again this is not set in stone. Find something that works for you. Find something that works in your agency. Another thing that we do is we have a three month sunset clause which means that let's say a referred client decides to stay with us for six months or a year. Well the original client that referred that client is going to get paid that discount off the monthly invoice for a period of three months. And so on month four they no longer get that discount applied. Likewise if the new client the referred client only stays with us for two months. Well the original client only gets the first two months off that invoice. And the second important thing here is that the clients need to be invoiced a minimum. In our case we usually say three thousand dollars but you could make that five thousand dollars as well. And the reason you want this is I mean obviously if you lend a referred client and they're only interested in a graphic design job that costs you know five hundred bucks you don't want to go in and give away twenty percent or twelve hundred dollars off a monthly invoice

## [09:10 - 12:13]

for that. So state that. Now when it comes to having this referral incentive I would recommend that when you decide to rule this out in your agency you include a clause about this in the contract. You don't even necessarily need to state the specific numbers if you don't feel comfortable or if you feel like that might change. You could list the numbers and state that it's due to or it is likely to change or could be changed I should say. But whatever you want to do whatever you do make sure that when you roll out a referral based incentive program you get that on paper somewhere. So I'll talk to you about that in a moment and how to communicate that to clients but having it in your contracts is a great thing to do as well. So when it comes to communicating this new program to your clients if it's a new client what you want to do is bring it up during the onboarding process and so typically this is when you've landed them as a client and you start discussing the next steps. However you need to feel the situation here but you could also use this as leverage for potential sales objections especially when it comes to pricing objections. So you go to pitch a lead the price and the lead says to you it's too expensive there's no way. Well once you go through the normal objections you could bring up the fact that hey look we've got this program and you were saying to me before that you have all these contacts and networks well look if you refer just three of them to me because we're going to be crushing results and you'll want to refer us you don't even end up paying for our services. So it's a great incentive it's a great hook right. And again you want to mention it when you raise the second invoice with that new client and it's a good time to do it when you raise invoices because they feel or they become acutely aware that they need to pay you they feel the sting of the invoice sometimes in a very nice way hopefully they're really happy to pay you but they still feel the sting and bringing it up at that time is a good time to do that because they're in that moment. Something to be aware of here though guys is if you're using zero or things like QuickBooks and you're raising the invoice and sending it to their accounts department as you normally would well the accounts person is not going to give two shits about a referral based program right she just doesn't care. So you need to make sure you send that in an email separately to the decision maker or whoever your point of contact is and with existing clients it's really as simple as

just sending them an email or bringing it up in the next meeting. You can't really change the unpredictable nature of referrals that's just the way referrals are but you can increase the number of referrals that you get through and that's a good thing for you and for your team because referrals are typically very easy to onboard. So a couple of things here guys we talked briefly about testimonials at the beginning and one of the things I just want to point out here is typically you're going to want to or you're going to feel compelled to bring up a referral based program to the clients that are in having a good campaign with you so having good results with you right makes sense you're not going to really feel comfortable broaching hey you know do you want to refer us to clients with a client whose campaign is absolutely bombing potentially it's not going to make a lot of sense right. However if this is a new client and you are crushing results for them and you're on a high with them your first focus before this referral based program needs to be testimonials. You still cannot solely depend on referrals we don't even though we have a lot of them coming through you're still going to need an automated lead gen system in place and those testimonials are gold mines to help you close those leads to increase that close rate so ask them for testimonial first and then follow this by saying hey by the way

## [15:19 - 17:27]

we have this program and here's the thing as well guys make sure you ask for those testimonials as soon as you get a high with them right so as long as they've been with you for at least three weeks and if you're crushing results ask them get them on a screen share with you a screen share interview where you can ask them the questions and then you can edit yourself out of it later on even write it out for them and say hey I know you're really really busy I took the liberty of writing this out for you to save you time would you be happy to you know for me to use this as a testimonial and the reason I say this is that Facebook is such a roller coaster ride so you could be doing so incredibly well with a client one minute and then Facebook will do something and it just tanks the next so you could have been on a really big high and you're saying hey I'm going to ask them for testimonial you know in a week or two or I'll get around to it next week and lo and behold next week things have turned the tides have shifted and you have to wait another two months to get back on track and get that testimonial and that's cost you potentially a lot of leads and a lot of money so ask for that testimonial in that pipeline as soon as you can so your task at hand right now for those of you that have existing clients and there's a great relationship in place you're getting them results or they love working with you roll out a referral based program send them an email about it tomorrow it doesn't take much work to do at all and start getting that process in your agency where it's just another source of leads coming your way right so if your automated lead gen system is ever drying up where it's ever slowed down for whatever reason you've still got something coming in because you're incentivizing that behavior you're training that behavior in your clients.
